frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Beta logon ko bura lagta hai, but sach yahi hai ki zamaana bahut kharab hai. Ladki ho toh thoda sambhal ke chalna padta hai.
B: Haan, itni raat ko akele nikalna, unknown cab lena, phir problem ho jaaye toh sabse pehle yahi poochna padta hai na ki thi kahan aur kis ke saath.
A: Main blame nahi kar rahi, bas practical baat hai—apni safety apne haath mein rakhni chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes restrictions on women instead of questioning male violence. It uses protective, practical-sounding advice and post-assault victim questioning ('kahan aur kis ke saath').
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
